title: CCC: first-run setup TUI + config management (openclaw-pattern)
description: Build a first-run setup experience for CCC that follows the openclaw pattern: console/TUI is trusted by physical access, web UI requires admin token. Setup is a guided conversation (not a CLI wizard) once the LLM provider is configured. All config stored in data/secrets.json (already the pattern). Goal: 'curl | sh' install + conversational TUI → fully configured CCC with no manual env var editing.
principles: Follow openclaw pattern: TUI trusted by console access, web UI requires admin token
TUI is a conversation, not a CLI prompt sequence — especially once LLM is wired
All persistent config lives in data/secrets.json (already established)
Everything configurable in TUI must also be readable/writable in the web UI
Skippable items are flagged with consequences, not blocked (e.g. 'no email = no user self-registration')

bootstrap_sequence.name: admin_token
bootstrap_sequence.description: Generate + display admin token. Console access = trusted. Store in secrets.json.

bootstrap_sequence.name: public_url
bootstrap_sequence.description: Set CCC_PUBLIC_URL. Required for email confirm links, agent callbacks, WebRTC signaling.

bootstrap_sequence.name: llm_provider
bootstrap_sequence.description: TokenHub URL + optional auth key. If host has a GPU, offer local vLLM as default. Unlocks conversational mode for remaining setup steps.
bootstrap_sequence.note: Skipping means setup remains CLI-style prompts, not conversational

bootstrap_sequence.name: email_provider
bootstrap_sequence.description: Resend API key, SMTP config, or Mailgun. Skip = DEV_MODE (console log only). Required for user self-registration and token reset.
bootstrap_sequence.note: Without email, admin must create all user accounts manually

bootstrap_sequence.name: messaging
bootstrap_sequence.description: Slack bot token + signing secret. Optional. Enables agent notifications and channel integration.

bootstrap_sequence.name: initial_users
bootstrap_sequence.description: Create user accounts. Admin sets username + email + optional phone (for Telegram/WhatsApp). Token shown once for copy. Phone number enables messaging channel setup later.

high_value: llm_provider (TOKENHUB_URL + TOKENHUB_ADMIN_TOKEN or local GPU URL)
optional: tunnel_config (TUNNEL_AUTH_KEYS, TUNNEL_USER, TUNNEL_PORT_START — Sweden GPU fleet)
minio_s3 (endpoint, access key, secret — file storage)

tui_design.mode: conversational once LLM is wired, structured prompts before
tui_design.trust_model: console access = admin, no token required for TUI
tui_design.storage: data/secrets.json (all config) + env var fallback for compat
tui_design.web_ui_parity: Settings page in CCC web UI mirrors all TUI config as read/write

user_management.admin_creates: username + email + optional phone, token shown once
user_management.self_register: username + email → email verification → token shown once (requires email provider)
user_management.phone_number: stored per-user, used for Telegram/WhatsApp channel setup later
user_management.agent_tokens: ADMIN_TOKEN env var stays as escape hatch, agents never go through email flow

out_of_scope: Automated SSL cert provisioning (use Caddy in front)
open_questions: TUI implementation: Node.js ink/blessed, or a simple readline conversation loop?
Is 'curl | sh' install realistic or should we target a package (deb/rpm/npm global)?
Should the TUI be a separate binary/script or part of the main ccc process (e.g. 'ccc setup')?
